outbound_official.note: No official Sierra Leone government outbound visa-list page located. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation (mofaic.gov.sl) and the Sierra Leone Immigration Department (slid.gov.sl) do not publish a list of countries where Sierra Leone ordinary passport holders may travel without a prior consular visa. mofaic.gov.sl was entirely unreachable (ECONNREFUSED / 502 Bad Gateway) during this research session and its indexed content consists only of bilateral-agreement news articles, not an outbound destination matrix. slid.gov.sl focuses exclusively on inbound immigration services. No page analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin was found on any Sierra Leone government domain (gov.sl). Rely on destination-side data.

name: Sierra Leone

visa_types.name: Landing Visa / Visa on Arrival (Tourist/Visitor)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay entry for tourism, family visits, religious travel, or academic purposes. Issued at designated ports of entry.

fee_local.note: Le800,000 single entry / Le1,200,000 multiple re-entry for non-AU nationals. Le250,000 single / Le375,000 multiple for non-ECOWAS AU nationals. Free for ECOWAS member nationals, diplomats, and UN Laissez-Passer holders. Fee structure published by SLID (March 2023). Original 2019 NTB policy quoted USD 80 (non-AU) / USD 25 (non-ECOWAS AU) - these USD amounts may represent the pre-2023 structure.

visa_types.notes: Now officially termed 'Landing Visa' on SLID website (slid.gov.sl/landing-visa/, published March 2023). VoA policy originally introduced 5 September 2019 per NTB. Available at Freetown International Airport, Jendema (Liberia border), and Gbalamuya (Guinea border). Extendable once in-country. Single or multiple re-entry options available.

visa_types.name: E-Visa (Tourist/Visitor)
visa_types.purpose: Advance online visa for tourism, family visits, or short-stay purposes for nationals not eligible for visa on arrival or ECOWAS free movement.
visa_types.notes: Applied for through the official Sierra Leone eVisa portal (evisa.sl), operated by the Government of Sierra Leone. Required for nationals not covered by ECOWAS free movement or the Landing Visa (VoA) policy. Specific fee, validity, and max-stay details are not published on a publicly accessible page of evisa.sl without beginning an application. Can also be obtained through Sierra Leone embassies and consulates abroad.

visa_types.name: Business Landing Visa / Visa on Arrival
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay entry for business meetings, negotiations, or commercial activities. Issued under the same VoA/landing visa framework as tourist visits.

fee_local.note: Same fee structure as tourist landing visa. Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple for non-AU nationals; Le250,000 single / Le375,000 multiple for non-ECOWAS AU nationals.

visa_types.notes: Business visits are covered under the same landing visa framework as tourism. NTB official policy (effective September 5, 2019) confirms VoA covers tourism, business, religious, academic, and family visits. Same fee structure applies. Extendable once in-country.

visa_types.name: Work Permit (Employment Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Authorises a foreign national to undertake employment in Sierra Leone for a Sierra Leonean employer or organisation.
visa_types.notes: Applied for through the Unified Permit portal (unifiedpermit.gov.sl). Mandatory as of January 1, 2026 - all applications and renewals must go through the online platform. Non-ECOWAS fee per SLIEPA FAQ: Employee 3,000,000 Leones; Self-Employed 3,600,000 Leones; renewal 855,000 Leones. ECOWAS nationals: Employee/Self-Employed 1,500,000 Leones. Also referred to as the Employment category under the Unified Permit system.

visa_types.name: Business Immigration Quota (BIQ) Permit
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign investors and business owners to reside and operate businesses in Sierra Leone, with an allocated immigration quota for key personnel.
visa_types.notes: One of the named categories under the Unified Permit system (unifiedpermit.gov.sl). BIQ allows companies to bring foreign workers up to a quota. Alongside this is the Company Quota (CQ) permit. Specific fees and validity periods are not published on the public-facing pages of unifiedpermit.gov.sl.

visa_types.name: Student Permit
visa_types.purpose: Authorises a foreign national to reside in Sierra Leone for the purpose of full-time study at an accredited institution.
visa_types.notes: Named as one of the categories in Sierra Leone's Unified Permit system. Applications and renewals must be submitted online via unifiedpermit.gov.sl as of January 1, 2026. Specific fee amounts, duration, and eligibility requirements are not published on publicly accessible pages of the unified permit portal.

visa_types.name: Dependant Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows family members (dependants) of foreign nationals holding a valid work, business, or resident permit to reside in Sierra Leone.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a named category in Sierra Leone's Unified Permit system. Intended for spouses and dependent children of permit holders. Applications submitted online via unifiedpermit.gov.sl. Specific eligibility, fees, and durations are not published on publicly accessible portions of the portal.

visa_types.name: GO-FOR-GOLD Permanent Residency Programme
visa_types.purpose: Grants immediate Permanent Residency to high-net-worth foreign investors who deposit gold bullion in Sierra Leone government vaults.
visa_types.notes: Officially announced by SLID in a January 2025 press release. No in-country residency requirement to acquire or retain PR. Includes an upgrade pathway to citizenship. Administered by SLID. Minimum investment amounts appear only on the private operator website (goforgold.center) and are not published on any official *.gov.sl domain. Programme status as of June 2026 is uncertain - SLID website does not actively feature current articles about this programme.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic/Service Passport Visa Exemption (China)
visa_types.purpose: Mutual visa exemption for holders of valid PRC diplomatic and service passports travelling to Sierra Leone on official duties.
visa_types.notes: Based on the Agreement Between China and Sierra Leone on Mutual Visa Exemption for Holders of Diplomatic and Service Passports, entered into force December 24, 2018. Covers diplomatic and service passports (including public affairs passports) of the PRC. Stays exceeding 30 days require registration with competent authorities. Diplomatic/consular mission members and accredited families are exempt for the duration of their accreditation. Ordinary PRC passport holders are NOT exempt; they qualify for VoA/landing visa under the BRICS category.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic/Service/Official Passport Visa Waiver (South Africa)
visa_types.purpose: Mutual visa waiver for holders of South African diplomatic, service, and official passports travelling to Sierra Leone.
visa_types.notes: Agreed effective November 30, 2023, announced during a courtesy call by South African High Commissioner H.E. Grace Jeanet Mason at MoFAIC Building, Freetown on November 2, 2023. Ordinary South African passport holders qualify for landing visa under the BRICS/AU category. Max stay duration for diplomatic/service/official holders not confirmed - mofaic.gov.sl was inaccessible (ECONNREFUSED) during June 2026 re-validation.

visa_types.name: ECOWAS Free Movement Right of Entry
visa_types.purpose: Visa-free entry and right of residence for up to 90 days for citizens of ECOWAS member states, per the 1979 ECOWAS free movement protocol.
visa_types.notes: Under ECOWAS Protocol A/P.1/5/79 dated May 29, 1979. Citizens of the 14 other ECOWAS member states (Benin, Burkina Faso, Cabo Verde, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Côte d'Ivoire, Liberia, Mali, Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, Togo) may enter without a visa and reside up to 90 days. Free landing visa per SLID (slid.gov.sl/landing-visa/, March 2023). Right to reside is granted but does not confer right to employment - a work permit is still required for employment.

visa_free.nationality: ECOWAS member state nationals (Benin, Burkina Faso, Cabo Verde, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Côte d'Ivoire, Liberia, Mali, Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, Togo)
visa_free.notes: Under ECOWAS Protocol A/P.1/5/79 dated 29 May 1979, citizens of ECOWAS member states may enter without a visa and reside for up to 90 days. Free landing visa issued at ports of entry per SLID (March 2023). Current ECOWAS membership is 15 states (Mauritania withdrew). Right to reside is granted but does not confer right to employment. Sierra Leone itself is excluded from this list as the destination country. Both the NTB official page (September 2019) and SLID landing-visa page (March 2023) confirm ECOWAS nationals as visa-free.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Landing visa (visa on arrival) per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019 and SLID landing-visa page (March 2023). Available at Freetown International Airport, Jendema (border with Liberia) and Gbalamuya (border with Guinea). Fee: Le800,000 single entry / Le1,200,000 multiple re-entry per SLID March 2023 (original NTB 2019 quoted USD 80). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.

All EU member state nationals receive landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019 and SLID landing-visa page (March 2023). Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.

Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019 and SLID landing-visa page (March 2023). Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.

All Commonwealth member state nationals receive landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee varies: non-ECOWAS AU Commonwealth members pay Le250,000 single / Le375,000 multiple; non-African Commonwealth members pay Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.

visa_on_arrival.nationality: Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) member country citizens (UAE, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman)
visa_on_arrival.notes: All GCC nationals receive landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.

visa_on_arrival.nationality: BRICS country citizens (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa)
visa_on_arrival.notes: All BRICS nationals receive landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee varies: South Africa pays Le250,000 single / Le375,000 multiple (AU non-ECOWAS); Brazil, Russia, India, China pay Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU) per SLID March 2023. Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country. Note: BRICS expanded post-2019 (Egypt, Ethiopia, Iran, UAE joined 2024) but the NTB policy lists the original 5 BRICS members explicitly - expanded members not confirmed on any official Sierra Leone source.

Indian ordinary passport holders receive landing visa as BRICS members per Sierra Leone NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019 and SLID (March 2023). Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country with Chief Immigration Officer approval. Valid for tourism, business, religious, academic, or family visits. Single or multiple entry available. Ports of entry: Freetown International Airport, Jendema (Liberia border), Gbalamuya (Guinea border). Airline aggregator claim of 'visa_free' is INCORRECT - entry requires a landing visa at port of entry.

visa_on_arrival.nationality: Non-ECOWAS African Union member state nationals
visa_on_arrival.notes: Non-ECOWAS AU nationals receive landing visa at Le250,000 single / Le375,000 multiple per SLID official policy (March 2023). Specific AU members (Botswana, Eswatini, Kenya, Lesotho, Malawi, Mauritius) receive free landing visa per bilateral agreements. Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.
Free landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019 and confirmed on SLID (March 2023). Citizens with bilateral reciprocal agreements receive free landing visas. Duration: 30 days.
Free landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Duration: 30 days.
Free landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019 and confirmed on SLID (March 2023). Duration: 30 days.
Free landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Duration: 30 days.
Free landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Duration: 30 days.
Free landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Duration: 30 days.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.

Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country. Note: Iran is also referenced in unverified sources as having a diplomatic/service passport exemption, but only ordinary-passport landing visa is confirmed on an official Sierra Leone government source.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.

Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019 (listed individually alongside EU; Norway is EEA but not EU). Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.

Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019 (listed as 'East Timor'). Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.

Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country. Note: Macau is a Special Administrative Region of China; this appears to apply to Macau SAR travel documents.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.
Landing visa per NTB official policy effective 5 September 2019. Fee: Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple (non-AU, per SLID March 2023). Duration: 30 days; extendable once in-country.

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not covered by ECOWAS free movement, landing visa (VoA), or bilateral visa-exemption
e_visa.notes: Nationals not covered by ECOWAS free movement or the September 2019 VoA/landing visa policy must obtain a visa in advance. The official eVisa portal (evisa.sl) is operated by the Government of Sierra Leone. SLID officially directs all advance visa applications to evisa.sl. Applications can also be submitted at Sierra Leone embassies or consulates abroad.

visa_policy.notes: Sierra Leone's visa admission framework has three tiers: (1) Visa-free for ECOWAS nationals under the 1979 ECOWAS Protocol - free landing visa at ports of entry per SLID March 2023; (2) Landing Visa / Visa on Arrival (VoA) introduced 5 September 2019, covering UK/EU/US/Commonwealth/GCC/BRICS nationals and numerous additional countries - fee structure updated by SLID (March 2023) to Le800,000 single / Le1,200,000 multiple for non-AU nationals, and Le250,000 single / Le375,000 multiple for non-ECOWAS AU nationals. Free landing visa for Botswana, Eswatini, Kenya, Lesotho, Malawi, Mauritius under bilateral agreements; (3) E-visa (advance application at evisa.sl) for all remaining nationalities. The SLID official page (slid.gov.sl/landing-visa/, published March 2023) is now the primary authoritative source for fees. The NTB page (ntb.gov.sl/visa-on-arrival-to-sierra-leone/) remains the primary source for the eligibility list (effective 5 September 2019). VoA is single or multiple entry, 1-month (30 days) max stay, extendable once. Ports of entry: Freetown International Airport, Jendema (Liberia border), Gbalamuya (Guinea border).

conditional_access.conditions: Agreement Between the Government of the People's Republic of China and the Government of the Republic of Sierra Leone on Mutual Visa Exemption for Holders of Diplomatic and Service Passports, entered into force 24 December 2018. Covers holders of valid diplomatic or service passports (including passports for public affairs) of the PRC. Stay must not exceed 30 days; stays exceeding 30 days require registration with competent authorities. Diplomatic/consular mission members and families holding qualifying passports are exempt for the duration of accreditation. Ordinary passport holders are NOT exempt - they qualify for landing visa under BRICS/non-AU category.

Sierra Leone and South Africa agreed on a mutual visa waiver for holders of Diplomatic, Service, and Official passports, effective 30 November 2023. Announced at MoFAIC Building, Tower Hill, Freetown on 2 November 2023 during a courtesy call by South African High Commissioner H.E. Grace Jeanet Mason. Ordinary South African passport holders qualify for landing visa under BRICS category. Max stay duration for diplomatic/service/official holders not confirmed on official source (mofaic.gov.sl was inaccessible at time of re-validation, June 2026).

cbi.program_name: Naturalization of Qualifying Foreign National Investors (GO-FOR-GOLD pathway)
cbi.notes: The Sierra Leone Immigration Department (SLID) issued an official press release on 2 January 2025 announcing 'groundbreaking measures aimed at facilitating the naturalization of qualifying high-net-worth foreign national investors.' The release references the GO-FOR-GOLD residency program launched December 2024, which includes an upgrade pathway to citizenship. A CBI programme therefore exists officially. However, the specific investment minimums, fees, and detailed program structure are NOT stated on any *.gov.sl page; those details appear only on the private commercial operator website goforgold.center (TG 2050 LTD). Therefore no financial options are recorded per hard rules. The SLID website as of June 2026 does not feature active articles on this programme, creating uncertainty about current status.

programs.name: GO-FOR-GOLD Permanent Residency Programme
programs.notes: Officially announced by SLID in January 2025 press release. Program grants immediate Permanent Residency through investment in Sierra Leonean gold bullion deposited in government vaults for 5 years. No in-country residency requirement to acquire or retain PR. Upgrade pathway to citizenship exists. Specific investment amounts appear only on private operator website goforgold.center - therefore min_amount is omitted per hard rules.

programs.name: Unified Permit - Residence Permit
programs.notes: Foreign nationals residing, studying, or working in Sierra Leone for more than 90 days require a Residence Permit issued by SLID. Categories: Business Immigration Quota (BIQ), Company Quota (CQ), Employment, Student, and Dependant. Applications fully online via unifiedpermit.gov.sl. Non-ECOWAS fees (from SLIEPA FAQ): Employee 3,000,000 Leones; Self Employed 3,600,000 Leones; renewal 855,000 Leones. ECOWAS citizens: Employee/Self Employed 1,500,000 Leones.

agreements: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States) free movement protocol
China–Sierra Leone Mutual Visa Exemption for Diplomatic and Service Passports (2018)
Sierra Leone–South Africa Mutual Visa Waiver for Diplomatic/Service/Official Passports (2023)

data_quality.validation_notes: Re-validated 2026-06-29 against official Sierra Leone government sources. PREVIOUS (2026-06-27): slid.gov.sl/landing-visa/ confirmed via search snippet; fee structure in Leones confirmed; NTB eligibility list confirmed unchanged. CURRENT (2026-06-29): (1) ELIGIBILITY LIST UNCHANGED - ntb.gov.sl/visa-on-arrival-to-sierra-leone/ re-fetched and confirmed LIVE (June 2026). Same nationality groups as 2019 NTB announcement: ECOWAS (visa-free), UK, EU, US, Commonwealth, GCC, BRICS, Lebanon, Iran, Cambodia, Vietnam, Singapore, Indonesia, Thailand, Turkey, Japan, South Korea, Norway, Israel, Bolivia, East Timor, Macau, Samoa, Tuvalu. No additions or removals from this list found on any official source. (2) SLID PAGES STILL EMPTY - slid.gov.sl/, slid.gov.sl/landing-visa/, slid.gov.sl/visa-on-arrival/, slid.gov.sl/traveling-to-sierra-leone/ all return empty content on direct WebFetch (June 2026). Fee structure in Leones (Le800,000/Le1,200,000 non-AU; Le250,000/Le375,000 non-ECOWAS AU) confirmed via web search snippet of SLID page - not a direct page load. (3) CBI/GO-FOR-GOLD - Searched for 'Citizenship (Special Naturalization) Regulation 2026' on official gov.sl domains; not found. No formal legal text or investment minimum confirmed on any *.gov.sl page. slid.gov.sl/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/PublicNotice-Naturalization.pdf returned empty. The SLID citizenship page and notices page also returned empty. CBI program entry kept as-is per preservation rule. (4) mofaic.gov.sl remains inaccessible - South Africa conditional_access entry preserved unchanged. (5) ntb.gov.sl/visa-information/ continues to return 404. (6) evisa.sl returns navigation shell only - no policy details. No changes made to any visa_policy access lists.
gaps: SLID landing-visa page content did not render to WebFetch despite being indexed by search engines - content was confirmed via Google search snippet only. Direct fetch returned empty. The fee information (Leones amounts) is taken from the search engine snippet/excerpt of the SLID page, not a full page load.
VoA/landing visa max stay: NTB page references '1 month'. SLID search snippet confirms 30-day equivalence. Recorded as 30 days.

Countries receiving free landing visa: NTB page lists Botswana, Eswatini, Kenya, Lesotho, Malawi, Mauritius. Wikipedia also lists Barbados, Fiji, Jamaica, Malaysia, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Sri Lanka, Trinidad and Tobago as receiving free VoA - these additional countries appear ONLY in Wikipedia, not on any official Sierra Leone government page, and are therefore not recorded.
mofaic.gov.sl was entirely inaccessible (ECONNREFUSED) during re-validation June 2026. South Africa diplomatic/service/official passport waiver max stay remains unconfirmed.

CBI/RBI investment amounts: Specific minimum investment figures appear only on private commercial operator websites (goforgold.center, slcbi.com), not on any official *.gov.sl domain. A public notice PDF (slid.gov.sl/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/PublicNotice-Naturalization.pdf) returned empty content on fetch.

CBI formal legal framework: Search for 'Citizenship (Special Naturalization) Regulation 2026' on gov.sl and parliament.gov.sl returned no matching official document. Program existence confirmed by SLID press release (January 2025) and Tourism Ministry citizenship conferral events, but formal legal text not accessible on any official domain.

Current CBI/GO-FOR-GOLD program status: The SLID website as of June 2026 does not actively promote or describe this programme on any fetchable page, creating uncertainty about whether it is still active and what its current terms are.
Dual citizenship: Sierra Leone's position on dual citizenship for CBI naturalized investors was not confirmed on any official source.

Conditional access - Iran diplomatic/service passport: Wikipedia states a max-stay-1-month visa exemption exists for Iranian diplomatic and service passport holders. Cannot be confirmed on any official Sierra Leone or Iranian government source - not recorded in conditional_access.

Conditional access - Rwanda: Wikipedia states a visa exemption agreement for diplomatic and service passports was signed August 2019 but has yet to be ratified. No official source found - not recorded.
Conditional access - Kazakhstan: Wikipedia lists a future agreement signed February 2017 for diplomatic/service passports, not yet in force. No official source found - not recorded.
e-Visa validity and duration: The evisa.sl portal does not publish a public-facing page listing visa types and specific durations without completing an application.

BRICS expanded post-2019 (Egypt, Ethiopia, Iran, UAE, and others admitted 2024). The original NTB policy listed the original 5 BRICS members. Whether the expanded BRICS members are also covered is not confirmed on any official Sierra Leone source.
Special status (OCI/diaspora): No official source was found granting special entry to OCI cardholders or any comparable diaspora/heritage status.
Credential-based entry: No official Sierra Leone source was found granting visa-free, VoA, or e-visa access based on the traveller holding a third-country visa or residence permit.
Unified Permit fee amounts in USD: Fees are published in Leones (Sierra Leone currency) on the SLIEPA FAQ. USD equivalents were not confirmed on any official source as of June 2026.
slid.gov.sl/visa-on-arrival/ and slid.gov.sl/traveling-to-sierra-leone/ listed in search results but direct fetch returned empty content (June 2026).
